commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore setup.sh boilerplate markers, add functional app-to-pgbouncer connectivity check in grader

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add chat command system with Spotify controls

Server connects to Twitch IRC as anonymous reader and watches for commands
in chat. Dashboard shows a Commands card with a Trigger button per command.

Commands:
- !song        — shows Spotify overlay for 10s regardless of mode
- !hidespotify — disables auto-show on track change
- !showspotify — re-enables 15s auto-show on track change

GET /command/:name lets dashboard buttons fire commands without chat.
Spotify overlay handles set_mode and show_now message types. New connections
receive current mode immediately on connect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use tight-fit rendering for composed components

Components in compositions now scale based on actual stroke bounds
instead of the full 1024x1024 canvas, eliminating visual gaps that
made left-right characters look like two separate glyphs. Also
applies tight-fit to donor sub-expressions (e.g. 丸 inside ⿰扌⿻九丶).

commit.message:
Track E: executeSequences_loop_history_valid — offset history validity through sequence loop (#880)

* feat: decompressBlocksWF_prefix — output prefix preservation through block loop

When decompressBlocksWF succeeds, every byte that was in the output buffer
before the call is present at the same index in the result. This is the
content-level counterpart to decompressBlocksWF_output_size_ge, establishing
that block decompression is append-only in both size and content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for decompressBlocksWF_prefix (#868)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Review: DeflateDynamicEmit.lean quality — eliminate maxRecDepth and linter pragmas (#869)

* refactor: remove linter.unusedSimpArgs pragma and fix unused simp args

Remove `set_option linter.unusedSimpArgs false` from
emitTokensWithCodes_wf_go. The `simp only [htok]` / `simp only [hflc]`
/ `simp only [hfdc]` calls after match branches used those hypotheses
unnecessarily — the match constructor iota-reduction happens without
them. Replace with `simp only []` where iota reduction is still needed,
and remove entirely where the match already reduced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove unnecessary maxRecDepth 2048 pragmas

Both `set_option maxRecDepth 2048` pragmas in DeflateDynamicEmit.lean
were no longer needed — the proofs compile at default depth (1024).
These were likely required by an older toolchain version.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: use dsimp for iota reduction, remove Header linter pragma

In emitTokensWithCodes_wf_go, replace `simp only []` (used for match
iota-reduction after case split) with the more idiomatic `dsimp only`.

Also remove the unnecessary `set_option linter.unusedSimpArgs false`
from writeDynamicHeader_wf in DeflateDynamicHeader.lean — no warnings
are produced without it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for DeflateDynamicEmit review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: prove offset history validity through executeSequences.loop

Add two new theorems to Zip/Spec/ZstdSequence.lean:

- `resolveOffset_history_valid_of_fst_ne_zero`: unifies the large and
  repeat history validity lemmas into a single theorem whose precondition
  matches the loop's `if offset == 0 then .error` guard.

- `executeSequences_loop_history_valid`: threads ValidOffsetHistory
  through the sequence execution loop by induction, applying the
  unified lemma at each step.

These are characterizing properties (level 2 spec quality) proving that
sequence execution preserves offset history validity — an invariant
independent of specific block content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for executeSequences_loop_history_valid (#870)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove duplicate service image background from hero, keep brick texture only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix emoji-to-text conversion for multi-codepoint sequences

The regex-based approach split ZWJ sequences (e.g. 👨‍🦰, 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦)
into individual codepoints, so they never matched in EMOJI_MAP.
Use Intl.Segmenter with grapheme granularity instead, which correctly
handles ZWJ, skin tone modifiers, and flag sequences.

commit.message:
feat: agent-scoped canary cohorts + overlap blocking (iv-f7gsz)

Scope canary baselines and samples to per-agent cohorts instead of
global metrics. Block same-agent concurrent canaries, warn on
cross-agent overlap. Existing canaries without cohort_key fall back
to global behavior.

- Schema migration: add project + cohort_key columns to canary table
- _interspect_compute_canary_baseline: 3rd param filters by agent
- _interspect_record_canary_sample: skip sessions outside cohort
- _interspect_check_canary_overlap: BLOCK same-agent, WARN cross-agent
- Wire overlap check + agent filter into all 3 override apply sites
- Enhanced verdict with cohort_key + specific overlap agent listing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: geomap now lights up — three root causes resolved

1. Header had <a> nested inside <a> (Link wrapping mythx link),
   causing React hydration mismatch that crashed the entire page tree.
   Moved "by Mythx" link outside the Next.js Link component.

2. styled-jsx <style jsx> in a "use client" component caused additional
   hydration mismatch. Moved keyframe animations to globals.css.

3. Country codes from DB were full names ("Germany") not ISO2 ("DE").
   Added reverse lookup (name → ISO2) in getCountryDistribution() so
   the GeoHeatmap's ISO2_TO_NUMERIC mapping works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

doc: summarize 11-PR batch — le_size capstone + content preservation + FSE validity (#878)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(odds): rewrite OddsPapi provider to use correct fixture-based API

OddsPapi uses a fixture-based API (/v4/fixtures + /v4/odds?fixtureId=X),
not sport-based endpoints. The old code called non-existent endpoints
(/sports/{key}/odds) resulting in 404s and zero odds enrichment.

- Rewrite oddspapi.ts with correct 2-step API: fetch fixtures → fetch odds
- Transform OddsPapi's nested bookmakerOdds format to The Odds API format
- Use 6-hour cache for fixtures to stay within 200 req/month limit
- Simplify buildOddsLookup() to use new OddsPapi sport-based getter
- Fix cron-jobs.ts to use updated provider exports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add containerized development environment for clean testing

Implement hybrid dev approach: tmux for development, containers for testing.

Docker/Podman Setup:
- Dockerfile: Base image with Rust + wrangler for workers
- Dockerfile.cli: Python CLI image with wrangler for D1 access
- docker-compose.yml: Orchestrates 6 services
  - db-init: Runs migrations, seeds database
  - webfinger, actor, inbox, outbox: Workers on ports 8787-8790
  - cli: Interactive shell for dais commands
- Shared D1 database via volume (all workers use same SQLite)

Scripts:
- scripts/seed-container-db.sh: Seed database in containers
- scripts/test-containers.sh: Run all tests in clean environment
  - Phase 1 tests (10 tests)
  - Phase 2 tests (10 tests)
  - Python unit tests (15 tests)
  - Supports --clean for fresh start

Makefile: Convenience commands
- make up/down: Start/stop services
- make test: Run all tests
- make test-clean: Fresh rebuild + test
- make seed: Seed database
- make shell: Open CLI shell
- make logs: Tail all logs
- make clean: Remove volumes (fresh start)

Benefits:
✅ Reproducible tests (same every time)
✅ Isolated state (no contamination)
✅ Easy reset (make clean && make up)
✅ CI/CD ready (same containers everywhere)
✅ No host dependencies (just Docker/Podman)

Updated DEVELOPMENT.md:
- Container workflow documentation
- Comparison: containers vs tmux
- Hybrid workflow recommendations
- Troubleshooting for containers

Use Cases:
- Development: ./scripts/dev-start.sh (tmux, hot reload)
- Testing: make test-clean (containers, clean slate)
- CI: make ci-test (automated testing)

Replace Azure DevOps pipeline with GitHub Actions

Migrate CI/CD from azure-pipelines.yaml to .github/workflows/ci.yml.

Build-and-test job runs on every push/PR to main: restore, build,
test (net8.0 + net10.0), and upload the .nupkg as an artifact.
Full git history is fetched for Nerdbank.GitVersioning.

Publish job runs only on push to main after tests pass, pushing the
package to NuGet.org via the NUGET_API_KEY secret with --skip-duplicate.

commit.message:
fix: accept x-vapi-secret token as fallback webhook authentication

Vapi's HMAC credential (credentialId) is not reliably sending the
x-vapi-signature header for inbound calls. Add support for Vapi's
simpler server.secret mechanism which sends x-vapi-secret header.

Auth priority:
  1. x-vapi-signature (HMAC-SHA256) — if present, verify HMAC
  2. x-vapi-secret (plain token)    — if present, verify token match
  3. Neither present                — reject

Also set server.secret on the Vapi assistant (isServerUrlSecretSet=true)
so all tool-call and status webhooks now include x-vapi-secret.
